title: FBN Holdings Plc Non-Core Income Drives 2023 Half Year Profitability Growth
description: FBN Holdings Plc (FBNH) published its unaudited 2023 half year financial statements, reporting a remarkable growth in its earnings per share by 234.8% as against N1.55/s in half year 2022. The group’s
html:
FBN Holdings Plc (FBNH) published its unaudited 2023 half year financial statements, reporting a remarkable growth in its earnings per share by 234.8% as against N1.55/s in half year 2022. The group’s earnings growth in the period was supported by the increase across the funded +69.3% and non-funded +114.0% income lines.

Interest income rose by 69.3% to N383.29 billion, partly reflecting the elevated yield environment as the group’s earnings yield settled at 9.4% relative to 7.5% in recorded in half year 2022. Specifically, the group recorded higher income from investment securities by 127.5%, loans and advances to banks stood at 53.8% and customers 53.3%.

The HoldCo’s profit before tax grew by 213.1% to N206.26 billion. FBNH’s ROAE and ROAA ultimately settled at 31.5% against 12.8% in 2022 and 3.0% against 1.2% in 2022, respectively.

Away from the elevated yield environment, market analyst attribute the higher funded income to the increase in FBNH’s interest earning assets,  loans and advances to customers 38.9% to N5.26 trillion and investments securities, which 39.8%  to N3.64 trillion as at half year of 2023.

Similarly, interest expense grew by 98.7% to N145.96 billion, triggered primarily by the 109.7% surge in the cost incurred on customers’ deposits. Despite the slight improvement in the group’s CASA mix of 81.5% in 2023 against 80.2% in 2022, investment analyst, highlight that the higher interest rate in the environment and the growth in deposits from customers which was 26.9% to N9.04 trillion spurred the higher costs on deposits.

The report shows that higher costs were incurred on the deposit from banks by 122.4% and borrowings by 24.3%. Subsequent to the faster growth in interest income than expenses, net interest income grew stronger by 55.2% to N237.33 billion.

Impressively, the Holdco’s non-interest income rose by 114.0% to N258.12 billion, driven by the spike in net gains on financial instruments by1938.4ppts to NGN229.74 billion, reflecting the substantial foreign exchange gains induced by the recent market liberalisation.

Additionally, FBNH recorded higher gains from fees and commissions by 1.7% to N58.37 billion and net gains on investment securities by 4.6% to N22.4 billion. The growth in the funded income supported by the significant growth in non interest income drove the increase in operating income by 73.9%  to N437.82 billion.

Further down, operating expenses rose by 24.5% following the increase in regulatory costs such as AMCON levy which stood at 18.4% to N62.12 billion and NDIC 6.0% to N7.26 billion and personnel expenses that stood at 17.9% to N65.22 billion.
